stops.name: Tirana
stops.notes: Arrived via British Airways and connected by bus to Himara.
stops.description: Albania's capital is chaotic and colorful in equal measure. The bus terminal is a crash course in Balkan travel; nothing is labeled, everything moves fast, and somehow it all works out.

stops.name: Himare
stops.notes: Four nights on the Albanian Riviera at The Sea Cave Camping. Beautiful coastline surrounded by limestone cliffs.
stops.description: The Albanian Riviera is largely undiscovered compared to the rest of the Mediterranean, with very few tourists, incredibly clear water, and some of the cheapest camping in Europe.

stops.name: Shkoder
stops.notes: Stayed in a hostel before heading into the mountains.
stops.description: One of Albania's oldest cities, sitting at the foot of the Albanian Alps with a relaxed pace that makes it easy to spend more time than planned. Rozafa Castle has some of the best views in the country.

stops.name: Shala River
stops.notes: Reached by boat through Koman Lake. Camped along the turquoise river.
stops.description: Known as the Blue Eye of Albania, the Shala River canyon is only reachable by boat through the Koman reservoir. It is one of the most scenic ferry rides in the Balkans and completely off the tourist radar.

stops.name: Valbona
stops.notes: Starting point for the Peaks of the Balkans trail. Alpine valley surrounded by peaks.
stops.description: Valbona valley is considered one of the last truly wild places in Europe. The Albanian Alps here are dramatic and largely unmapped, which is exactly what makes the Peaks of the Balkans trail so rewarding.

stops.name: Cerem
stops.notes: First full day of hiking out of Valbona. Remote village in the Albanian Alps.
stops.description: Deep in the Albanian Alps with no roads and no phone signal. Guesthouses here are family-run operations that have been hosting hikers for generations. Simple, warm, and exactly what you need after a full day on the trail.

stops.name: Doberdol
stops.notes: High mountain pasture settlement near the Kosovo border. Ridge views in all directions.
stops.description: A high alpine settlement sitting near the triple border zone where Albania, Kosovo, and Montenegro converge. On a clear day you can see all three countries from the ridge above the village.

stops.name: Milishevc
stops.notes: Crossed into Kosovo on the trail. High valley with views back into Albania.
stops.description: Kosovo is one of the youngest countries in the world, having declared independence in 2008. Most people pass through without stopping. Hiking in from Albania is one of the more unusual ways to arrive.

stops.name: Babino Polje
stops.notes: One of the most isolated stops on the route. Wild camping in the Kosovo highlands.
stops.description: One of the most remote overnight points on the entire Peaks of the Balkans circuit. No guesthouses, no infrastructure, just open highland terrain and a sky full of stars.

stops.name: Plav
stops.notes: Descended into Montenegro beside a glacial lake. Breakfast included at Guest House Erdan.
stops.description: Plav sits beside a glacial lake at 900 meters elevation, surrounded by peaks on all sides. It's one of Montenegro's least visited towns, which makes arriving on foot from Kosovo feel like a genuine discovery.

stops.name: Vusanje
stops.notes: Last stop in Montenegro before crossing back into Albania. Breakfast included.
stops.description: A tiny village at the end of a dead-end valley, squeezed between Montenegro and Albania. The Peaks of the Balkans trail passes directly through. Without the trail, almost nobody would come here.

stops.name: Theth
stops.notes: End of the Peaks of the Balkans trail. Traditional Albanian village with a waterfall nearby.
stops.description: Theth was completely cut off from the outside world for most of the 20th century due to Albania's isolation under communism. The stone lock-in tower in the village center was used to shelter men during blood feuds, a tradition that persisted into the 1990s.

stops.name: Shkoder
stops.notes: Back in Shkoder for the final night after completing the trail.
stops.description: Coming back to Shkodër after a week in the mountains feels like returning to civilization. The same streets that felt unremarkable on the way in feel like a luxury after seven days of guesthouse bunks and trail food.

stops.name: Tirana
stops.notes: Departure day. Flight from Tirana to Budapest at 1:10pm.
stops.description: Albania was one of the most isolated countries in the world until 1991. Visiting now feels like catching a place mid-transformation, before it fully changes.
